title: ferray-ufunc: no ufunc object — functions are standalone, not composable abstractions
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's ufunc is a first-class object (PyUFuncObject) with identity, nin/nout, registered loops per dtype, and methods: .reduce(), .accumulate(), .outer(), .at(), .reduceat(). This means np.add.reduce(arr) and np.add.outer(a,b) work automatically for any ufunc. ferray-ufunc has standalone functions (add, multiply, etc.) plus manually-written add_reduce, add_accumulate, multiply_outer. There's no generic ufunc type that composes reduction/accumulation/outer-product for free. A Ufunc<F> struct with registered identity element and methods would eliminate the need to hand-write reduce/accumulate/outer for each operation and enable user-defined ufuncs.
